name: Sierra del Camocho y Santa Bárbara — circular desde Plasencia
region_intro: Jogging around Plasencia offers diverse landscapes, with the Jerte River providing a scenic backdrop for many routes. The region features accessible urban green spaces, dedicated river paths, and more challenging trails in surrounding natural areas. Runners can explore varied terrain, from mostly paved surfaces along riverwalks to paths with elevation gains in the nearby hills. This combination makes Plasencia an excellent destination for a range of running experiences. Best jogging routes around Plasencia The most popular jogging route is Paseo Fluvial de Plasencia – circular by the Jerte river, a 5.1 miles (8.2 km) trail that takes 51 minutes to complete. This route follows the course of the Jerte River, offering a pleasant and accessible run. Another top favourite among local runners is Puente de San Lázaro by the Paseo Fluvial Plasencia, Aguas Abajo — circular by the Pantano de Plasencia, a difficult 9.5 miles (15.3 km) path. This route provides a longer, more challenging experience with views of the Pantano de Plasencia. Local runners also love the Plasencia Riverwalk Trail, a 7.5 miles (12.1 km) trail leading through the Jerte River area, often completed in about 1 hour 15 minutes. This path offers an accessible option with mostly paved surfaces. Jogging around Plasencia is defined by river routes, urban green spaces, and surrounding natural areas with varied terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy jogs to more demanding runs. The routes in Plasencia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 14 reviews. More than 600 runners have used komoot to explore Plasencia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Plasencia?
A: Plasencia offers a wide variety of running experiences, with over 50 routes available. These range from easy, flat paths to more challenging trails with significant elevation changes, catering to all fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails in Plasencia?
A: You'll find diverse terrain, from mostly paved surfaces along the Jerte River, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990933871/paseo-fluvial-de-plasencia-circular-by-the-jerte-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paseo Fluvial de Plasencia&lt;/a&gt;, to natural trails with varied elevation in the surrounding areas. Some routes, such as those near the Monfragüe Biosphere Reserve, offer more rugged paths and inclines.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners or casual jogs in Plasencia?
A: Yes, Plasencia has several easy running routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed jog. Many of the riverwalks, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990933871/paseo-fluvial-de-plasencia-circular-by-the-jerte-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paseo Fluvial de Plasencia – circular by the Jerte river&lt;/a&gt;, offer mostly flat and accessible paths. There are at least 4 easy routes available.

Q: Can I find longer, more challenging running routes in the Plasencia area?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a challenge, there are 10 difficult routes available. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1295180443/puente-de-san-lazaro-by-the-paseo-fluvial-plasencia-aguas-abajo-circular-by-the-pantano-de-plasencia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de San Lázaro by the Paseo Fluvial Plasencia, Aguas Abajo — circular by the Pantano de Plasencia&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 9.5-mile path offering a more demanding experience with views of the Pantano de Plasencia.

Q: Are there running routes in Plasencia that feature waterfalls or dramatic natural scenery?
A: Yes, the areas surrounding Plasencia are rich in natural beauty. For dramatic scenery and waterfalls, consider trails like the Garganta de las Nogaleas Trail or the Cascada del Caozo Trail in the Valle del Jerte. The Monfragüe Biosphere Reserve also offers routes like the Garganta del Fraile Loop with panoramic viewpoints and waterfalls.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or landmarks I can see while running near Plasencia?
A: Many routes offer stunning views. You can run along the Jerte River, passing by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5135059" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Lázaro Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. For panoramic vistas, the Sanctuary of Our Lady of the Port provides excellent views over Plasencia and the Jerte Valley. Within the city, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6517887" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor de Plasencia&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6517874" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plasencia Cathedral&lt;/a&gt; are also notable landmarks.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Plasencia?
A: Yes, many routes in Plasencia are circular, providing a convenient way to start and end your run in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990933871/paseo-fluvial-de-plasencia-circular-by-the-jerte-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paseo Fluvial de Plasencia – circular by the Jerte river&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1295154575/via-verde-por-el-puente-de-hierro-circular-desde-plasencia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde por el Puente de Hierro — circular desde Plasencia&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the running trails in Plasencia suitable for families?
A: Many of Plasencia's river routes and urban green spaces are family-friendly, offering accessible and relatively flat paths. The Parque de la Isla, a river island along the Jerte, is a great urban oasis for families to enjoy a jog or walk together.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running routes around Plasencia?
A: Generally, many outdoor trails in natural areas around Plasencia are suitable for dogs, especially those away from crowded urban centers. Riverwalks and green spaces often welcome well-behaved dogs on a leash. Always check local regulations for specific parks or protected areas.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Plasencia?
A: The running routes in Plasencia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 14 reviews. Runners often praise the scenic river views, the variety of terrain, and the accessibility of routes for different skill levels.

Q: Are there running routes that follow the Jerte River?
A: Yes, the Jerte River is a central feature for many running routes in Plasencia. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990933871/paseo-fluvial-de-plasencia-circular-by-the-jerte-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paseo Fluvial de Plasencia&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1295142952/plasencia-riverwalk-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plasencia Riverwalk Trail&lt;/a&gt; are excellent options that closely follow the river, offering pleasant, mostly paved surfaces and picturesque views.

Q: What are the options for parking near running trails in Plasencia?
A: Parking is generally available near popular starting points for river routes and urban trails. For routes extending into natural parks or reserves, designated parking areas are usually provided at trailheads. It's advisable to check specific route details for the most convenient parking options.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through unique forest areas?
A: Yes, the region offers routes through characteristic Extremaduran dehesa forests. The Vía Verde Bazagona, an old railway line, provides a peaceful running experience through these unique wooded landscapes, offering a different kind of scenery compared to the riverwalks.
tips: From this point before starting to climb towards the Barrio de Santa Teresa you can see a set of views of the city's cathedral!
Santa Bárbara is part of the southern side of the Sierra del Camocho and from where you can see good views of both Plasencia and the center of Malpartida de Plasencia to the south.

waypoints.name: View of Plasencia and Malpartida de Plasencia from Santa Bárbara
